content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two integers N,SN, SN,S.

Consider an array AAA of size NNN such that A[i]=iA[i]=iA[i]=i for every 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

You are required to find any position x(1≤x≤N)x(1\leq x \leq N)x(1≤x≤N) such that :

(A[1]+A[2]+...A[x−1])+(A[x+1]+A[x+2]+...A[N])=S(A[1]+A[2]+...A[x-1]) + (A[x+1]+A[x+2]+...A[N]) = S(A[1]+A[2]+...A[x−1])+(A[x+1]+A[x+2]+...A[N])=S

If there are multiple such positions xxx, print any such position. If there is no such position, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, two integers N,SN, SN,S.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the required position or −1-1−1 in case there is no such position.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and S.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line: the position x (1 ≤ x ≤ N) such that the sum of all array elements except A[x] equals S, or -1 if no such position exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=4, total=10, x=10-8=2 (valid). For N=5, total=15, x=15-10=5 (valid). For N=2, total=3, x=3-3=0 (out of range) so -1.

hidden.label: edge case where S equals total sum
general case with solution at last position

solution.approach: The array is defined as A[i] = i, so its total sum is total = N·(N+1)/2. The required condition is total - x = S, which directly gives x = total - S. The answer exists iff 1 ≤ x ≤ N. Thus for each test case we compute total using 128‑bit arithmetic (or arbitrary precision), compute x, and check the bounds.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
